title: On the Genealogy of Morality - Essay I - Sections 10-11 - Ressentiment
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain reactive bitterness and moral inversion, not to demean struggling people.
misreading_risk: Can be misused as a lazy insult instead of a precise moral-psychological diagnosis.
raw_text:
Ressentiment in Nietzsche is not just anger. It is a reactive, lingering hostility that cannot act directly, so it reinterprets weakness as virtue and strength as evil. This creates a moral reversal: what one cannot be or do becomes morally condemned.

For this project, this source is useful when a user turns frustration, envy, or impotence into moral accusation. It should be used carefully and not as a cheap label for ordinary disagreement.
